meta.title: PHP-FPM + OpenTelemetry Tracing Pipeline
meta.description: How Nginx round-robins across PHP-FPM instances, how OTel instruments individual PHP function spans, and how span batches travel through a collector chain with tail-based sampling to an observability platform.
Reverse proxy and load balancer. Distributes FastCGI requests across PHP-FPM instances using round-robin.
meta.notes: upstream php_fpm { server fpm1:9000; server fpm2:9000; } — round-robin by default

meta.description: PHP-FPM worker pool, instrumented with ext-opentelemetry. Spans are batched by BatchSpanProcessor and exported via OTLP/gRPC.
Span for AuthController::handle() — records route, user ID, and permission check result.
meta.notes: Kind: INTERNAL. Parent: HTTP server span.

meta.description: Span for PDO::query() — records db.system, db.statement, and row count.
Span for Response::render() — records template name and render time. Closing this span completes the trace.
meta.notes: Kind: INTERNAL. Last child span in the trace.

meta.description: Second PHP-FPM worker pool. Shares the same OTel config as Instance 1 but produces its own independent traces.
Span for Redis::get() — records cache key, hit/miss result, and latency.
Span for outbound HttpClient::post() — records URL, method, and HTTP status code.
meta.notes: Kind: CLIENT. Propagates W3C traceparent header to downstream service.

meta.description: OpenTelemetry Collector in agent mode. Receives OTLP/gRPC from all FPM instances, batches, and forwards to the gateway collector.
meta.notes: Pipeline: otlp receiver → batch processor → otlp exporter. Deployed as sidecar or DaemonSet.

meta.description: Central OTel Collector with tail-based sampling. Holds spans in memory until the root span arrives, then applies sampling policy based on error status and latency.
meta.notes: tailsampling processor: errors → 100%; duration > 1s → 50%; healthy fast traces → 10%.

meta.description: Generic observability backend (Jaeger, Honeycomb, Grafana Tempo, or Datadog). Receives OTLP-formatted traces over HTTPS.
meta.notes: Configured as otlphttp/platform exporter in the gateway collector.

steps.name: Overview
steps.title: PHP-FPM + OTel Observability Pipeline
steps.description: Nginx round-robins HTTP requests across two PHP-FPM instances. Each FPM process instruments individual PHP function calls as OTel spans, batches them to a local OTel Agent, which forwards to a central Collector for tail-based sampling before export to the observability platform.

steps.name: HTTP request
steps.title: HTTP request arrives at Nginx
steps.description: A GET /api/users/42 request hits Nginx. The upstream block has two FPM backends configured with round-robin. Nginx selects Instance 1 for this request.

annotations.text: GET /api/users/42 → upstream php_fpm { server fpm1:9000; server fpm2:9000; } → next = fpm1

steps.name: Route → FPM1
steps.title: Nginx forwards request to PHP-FPM Instance 1
steps.description: Nginx sends the request via FastCGI to Instance 1. The OTel SDK opens a root HTTP server span for this request, recording the method, route, and incoming traceparent header.

steps.name: Auth span
steps.title: PHP-FPM 1: auth.check span opened and closed
steps.description: The OTel SDK auto-instruments AuthController::handle(). A child span is opened, the permission check runs, and the result is recorded as an attribute before the span is closed.

steps.name: DB query span
steps.title: PHP-FPM 1: db.query span captured by PDO instrumentation
steps.description: PDO is auto-instrumented by opentelemetry-auto-pdo. The SELECT statement, database system, and row count are recorded as span attributes before the span closes.

steps.name: Render span
steps.title: PHP-FPM 1: template.render span closes — trace complete
steps.description: Template rendering completes. Its child span is closed, then the root HTTP server span is also closed, finalising the full trace. All 3 child spans are now queued in the BatchSpanProcessor.

steps.name: Batch export
steps.title: FPM 1 batch-exports 3 spans to OTel Agent
steps.description: The BatchSpanProcessor flushes its queue. The 3 completed spans are serialised to OTLP protobuf and sent to the local OTel Collector Agent on port 4317 over gRPC.

steps.name: Round-robin → FPM2
steps.title: Second request round-robins to PHP-FPM Instance 2
steps.description: The next incoming request is dispatched to Instance 2. FPM2 instruments a different code path — a Redis cache lookup and an outbound HTTP call to a payment service — producing spans with different attributes but the same trace structure.

annotations.text: Nginx upstream: next = fpm2 (round-robin) → OTel root span opened

steps.name: FPM2 batch export
steps.title: FPM 2 exports its span batch to OTel Agent
steps.description: Instance 2 flushes its BatchSpanProcessor. The 3 spans (root + cache.get + http.client) are exported to the same OTel Agent endpoint. The agent now holds completed traces from both FPM instances.

steps.name: Agent → Collector
steps.title: OTel Agent forwards span batches to Collector Gateway
steps.description: The Agent's batch processor groups spans from both FPM instances and forwards them upstream to the central OTel Collector Gateway via OTLP/gRPC. The agent applies minimal processing — batching and gzip compression only.

steps.name: Tail sampling
steps.title: Gateway applies tail-based sampling policy
steps.description: The tailsampling processor waits for the root span before evaluating the full trace. It applies policy rules in order: errors always sampled at 100%, slow traces over 1s at 50%, healthy fast traces at 10%.

annotations.text: tailsampling: trace_4bf9 → 23ms, status=200, no errors → policy: 10% → SAMPLED

steps.name: Export to platform
steps.title: Sampled trace exported to Observability Platform
steps.description: The sampled trace is serialised to OTLP/HTTP and exported to the observability backend. All 4 spans are visible in the platform's trace view, linked by their shared trace ID and parent-child span relationships.
